data.body: PASS 1 (criterion T1, prior-art/novel-architecture overlay) — CLEAN, no findings. No bright-line fires for this close-out ticket: emptying a ruff per-file-ignores allowlist, re-validating `# noqa` reasons, documenting a convention in CLAUDE.md, and a final make-lint/test gate are all well-trodden lint-enforcement and docs-cleanup patterns — no external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth surface, novel architectural pattern, performance target, or migration. Affirmed: (a) no reinvention/repackaging — it leans on existing ruff per-file-ignores + the established named-logger/observability-floor convention; (b) no novel pattern requiring Rule-of-Three justification; (c) no unverified third-party capability assertions. T1 PASS.
